chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D – 4816 of 2021 Order with signature of Judge(s) 1. For order on office objection 2. For hearing of Misc. No.19733/2021 (Stay) 3. For hearing of main case 21.09.2021 Mr. Asadullah Bhanbro, advocate and Mr. Abdul Basit Buriro, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Ali Safdar Depar, AAG Mr. Menghraj Mal, Research Economist, Finance Department, Government of Sindh. Mr. Abdul Jabbar Shahani, Litigation Officer, School Education & Literacy Department, Government of Sindh, Karachi Mr. Muhammad Nadeem, Deputy Director SEF. ----------------------------------- Malik Naeem Iqbal advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.3 and 4 along with letters of authorization, taken on record. Amicus Malik Altaf Hussain has submitted arguments, taken on record, copy whereof provided to learned counsel for the petitioner. At the outset, counsel for the respondents seeks time to submit profile of Abdul Jabbar (respondent No.4) as he is working as Regional Director HR though he was appointed as Subject Specialist. Pursuant to earlier order, Malik Naeem Iqbal advocate submitted report on behalf of respondents No.3 and 4, which is appended with certain orders and that report also includes scholarship provided by SEF by accommodating the SEF students in private and semi-private schools. Further he has contended that SEF will conduct third party assessment test on two years basis from any reputable institution like IBA Sukkur and verify the caliber of education provided by the proprietors under the SEF Act. With regard to salary of the teaching staff and criterion of recruitment of teaching staff, counsel contends that they will consider this aspect in new contract.

Further he has contended that SEF will conduct third party assessment test on two years basis from any reputable institution like IBA Sukkur and verify the caliber of education provided by the proprietors under the SEF Act. With regard to salary of the teaching staff and criterion of recruitment of teaching staff, counsel contends that they will consider this aspect in new contract. Secretary Finance and Secretary Schools shall submit report in terms that whether they are receiving funds from World Bank as per earlier MOU signed for the education to the students of families, who are under poverty line. They shall submit complete breakup of five years whether those funds were used in SEF or utilized in any other projects as well. To come up on 08.10.2021. JUDGE JUDGE Zahid/*
